title: ENG6-UG-VI-MLA-TEST
readingMaterial:
# Understanding MLA 9th Edition

MLA (Modern Language Association) style is a system used for writing research papers, essays, and academic projects, especially in literature and humanities. The latest version is the 9th edition. It helps writers present their ideas clearly and give proper credit to sources.

One important rule in MLA is **in-text citation**. When a writer uses someone else's ideas, words, or information, they must mention the author's last name and page number in brackets. For example: (Shakespeare 45). This helps readers find the exact source.

At the end of the paper, writers must include a **Works Cited** page. This page lists all the sources used in the paper. The title “Works Cited” should be centered at the top of the page.

MLA follows a standard format. The paper should be typed in **Times New Roman**, size 12, with double spacing. Margins should be one inch on all sides. The first line of each paragraph should be indented.

In MLA 9th edition, every source entry follows a general pattern:
**Author. Title of Source. Title of Container, Other Contributors, Version, Number, Publisher, Publication Date, Location.**

A **container** is the larger work where the source is found. For example, an article may be inside a journal, or a poem may be inside an anthology.

Book titles are written in *italics*, while titles of articles, poems, or short stories are placed inside quotation marks.

MLA also encourages writers to include URLs for online sources, but “https://” is not always necessary. If a source has a DOI (Digital Object Identifier), it is preferred over a URL.

Plagiarism means using another person's work without giving credit. MLA rules help prevent plagiarism by showing exactly where ideas and quotations come from.

MLA style makes academic writing more organized, clear, and trustworthy. It allows readers to check sources easily and understand where information has been taken from.

questions.text: What does MLA stand for?
MLA style is mainly used in?
What is the latest MLA version mentioned in the passage?
Why is MLA style important?
options: To organize writing and give credit to sources

questions.text: What is an in-text citation used for?
options: To show the source of borrowed ideas

questions.text: Which is the correct MLA in-text citation example?
What is the name of the page listing all sources?
Where should the title “Works Cited” be placed?
Which font is recommended in MLA?
What font size does MLA require?
MLA papers should be?
How wide should the margins be?
In MLA, book titles are written in?
Short story titles are written in?
What is a “container” in MLA?
options: The larger work in which a source appears

questions.text: Which is preferred in online citations if available?
What does DOI stand for?
What is plagiarism?
options: Copying another person's work without credit

questions.text: MLA helps prevent plagiarism by?
The overall purpose of MLA style is to?
options: Make academic writing clear, organized, and trustworthy
